subject: Sociology
title: Gender and Global Inequality
description: A 60-second lesson on Gender and Global Inequality.

hook.text: Women do 75% of unpaid work globally. They earn less, own less, and hold fewer seats of power everywhere.

buildup.text: Gender inequality intersects with global inequality: women in poor nations face compounded disadvantages.

discovery.text: Girls' education is the strongest development tool known. Educated women have fewer children and earn more.

twist.text: Globalization brings progress and exploitation. Factory jobs empower some women while trapping others in sweatshops.

climax.text: Gender equality isn't just a women's issue. It's a development issue that shapes outcomes for entire societies.

punchline.text: Empower women and nations rise with them.

quiz.question: Why is girls' education considered a key development tool?
options: Educated women earn more and have fewer children, improving outcomes
